EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An atmospheric river brought periods of moderate to heavy rain and snow and strong gusty winds to the higher elevations of Lemhi County between Jan 17 and 18. This led to the first avalanche cycle south of Lost Trail Pass.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A foreman with the Idaho Transportation Department reported that 13 avalanches occurred during this period. One of them crossed both lanes of US-93 at milepost 349.5. and was 12 feet high, 110 feet long and 12 feet wide. It took around 1 hour to clear one lane and another 2 hours to clear the remaining snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Much of southeast Idaho received severe thunderstorm winds and large hail on June 1st including a boating accident on Bear Lake with 4 fatalities from the severe thunderstorm winds capsizing a boat.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Between 4:30 and 6:30 on 6/1/2015 the Franklin County Sheriff's Office received several reports of power lines down with either broken power poles or trees fallen on lines, or power poles and lines fallen on houses.  Rocky Mountain Power and Utah Power are still working on restoring power, however, many residents are still without power.  Much of the damage was isolated to the West Side of Franklin County.  We had one report of a man electrocuted by a downed line, however, he was treated on scene by EMS and released.  We had many trees blocking roads which have now been removed, and other reports of property damage due to wind including one cargo trailer which was blown over.   We appreciate the help from the Preston Police Department, Franklin County Fire, EMS, Search & Rescue and Road & Bridge, Idaho State Police and Idaho State Fish & Game who all helped cover different calls.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The cause of the Cape Horn fire is still under investigation. Media reports have speculated it may have been started by a signal flare that was fired by a boater in distress and the wind blew the flare into the timber. The date of origin is Junday July 5,2015 at approximately 12:28 PM and the location is Cape Horn on the southern end of Lake Pend Oreille, IDaho. The fire burned 1,326 acres as of Friday July 17,2015.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wildfire that grew to 10,451 acres started on August 24th due to a lightning strike.   The fire was located about 17  miles west of Challis.  A type 3 team was deployed and the following closures occurred:  Bayhorse Roiad, Trealor Creek Road, Custer Motor Way, Garden Creek Road, Kinnikinic Creek Road, Mill Creek Campground, Mosquito Flat Campground, and Eight Mile Campground.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wildfire that grew to 10,451 acres started on August 24th due to a lightning strike.   The fire was located about 17  miles west of Challis.  A type 3 team was deployed and the following closures occurred:  Bayhorse Roiad, Trealor Creek Road, Custer Motor Way, Garden Creek Road, Kinnikinic Creek Road, Mill Creek Campground, Mosquito Flat Campground, and Eight Mile Campground.   The fire continued well into September before complete containment.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An exceptionally strong atmospheric river brought up to 3 inches of precipitation and well-above normal temperatures to the region. Record-breaking temperatures into the 50s occurred in many valley locations. Salmon experienced flooding due to snowmelt and rain runoff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Temperatures up to 48 F on February 6th, 2015 and above-freezing temperatures near 44 F overnight led to rapid snow melt which caused Salmon, ID area creeks and rivers to rise rapidly on February 7th. Temperatures reached up to 56 F that day, with rain runoff further complicating issues. The main flooding occurred near Kitz Creek and its confluence with the Salmon River. Four homes sustained minor flood damage in Salmon, ID. Additionally, the Salmon County Airport was shutdown for the day due to water on the runway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A four day interval in which a series of warm weather systems with very well maintained moisture feeds from the subtropics slowly moved through North Idaho. These systems brought considerable rainfall to the mountains and valleys and caused much of the mid-slope elevation snow to melt. The resulting runoff caused some streams and rivers to flood in addition to some debris flows, mudslides, and minor street flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Boundary County Emergency Manager reported water ponding on or flowing across numerous roads due to heavy rain and melting snow. Ruby Creek and Deep Creek went over their banks into the flood plain. Water was over Highland Flats Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A four day interval in which a series of warm weather systems with very well maintained moisture feeds from the subtropics slowly moved through North Idaho. These systems brought considerable rainfall to the mountains and valleys and caused much of the mid-slope elevation snow to melt. The resulting runoff caused some streams and rivers to flood in addition to some debris flows, mudslides, and minor street flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An Emergency Manager reported the debris flow on Highway 95 east of Mount Hall Elementary School 1 mile East Southeast of Copeland, Idaho.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across parts of Southwest Idaho overnight on the 18th through the 19th leading to flash flooding including a vehicle swept away by a debris flow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: KTVB received reports from the Owyhee County Sheriff's office that a vehicle had been swept away by flash flooding and a debris flow near Givens Hot Springs in Northwest Owyhee County at around 6:30 AM MDT. Idaho Transportation Department reported a rock slide on U. S. Highway 95 at mile marker 13 and occurred at approximately 2:39 PM MDT.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: This complex of lightning caused wildfires blackened 20,296 Acres according to the last report from October 7,2015.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An intense surface low pressure system moved from west to east along the Southern British Columbia border and dragged a cold front through as well. The resulting high winds brought about substantial damage across North Idaho....and resulted in widespread power outages and is noted as the most damage seen by Avista utilities from a natural disaster in its 126-year history with an estimated 180,000 Avista utilities customers without power at peak after the windstorm. One indirect fatality from the storm was reported in North Idaho. A state disaster declaration prepared for the four Idaho counties of Benewah, Bonner, Boundary and Kootenai included an initial damage estimate of $2.6 million.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported a wind gust of 60 mph 5 miles north of Mohler, Idaho.

The resulting high winds brought about substantial damage across North Idaho....and resulted in widespread power outages and is noted as the most damage seen by Avista utilities from a natural disaster in its 126-year history with an estimated 180,000 Avista utilities customers without power at peak after the windstorm. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong jet aloft with a core wind speed of 170+ mph helped drive a fast moving cold front which brought high winds to many locations across the Northern Rockies. Snow above 5500 feet lead to winter storm conditions for high passes and backcountry users (hunting season still in progress).  Lower atmosphere standardized wind anomalies ranged between +3 and +4 for this time of year, indicating a once-in-10-year wind event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A barn, with roof dimensions of approximately 40 by 50 feet, was blown off and traveled 100 to 150 feet to the east-southeast blocking both lanes of McIver Road.  Based on the damage, the wind gusts could have been 75 mph.

Lower atmosphere standardized wind anomalies ranged between +3 and +4 for this time of year, indicating a once-in-10-year wind event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Savage pass SNOTEL at 6200 ft reported 6 inches of new snow overnight, with heavy snow from 11pm to 1am 11/18. Nearby RAWS sensors showed wind gusts of 40 to 60 mph, making for blizzard-like conditions for anyone in the high terrain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weather disturbance went through North Idaho and produced heavy snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Coop observer reported 16 inches of snow in south southeast Clark Fork, Idaho. Power outages were reported in the region.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An intense surface low pressure system moved from west to east along the Southern British Columbia border and dragged a cold front through as well. The resulting high winds brought about substantial damage across North Idaho. In addition heavy rain over the two days preceding and occurring with the windstorm brought about river rises, mudslides, and debris flows.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public near Clark Fork, Idaho reported that Lightning creek is at bank-full with a video posted to social media.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weather disturbance moved down in a colder northwest to southeast trajectory and brought snow to many portions of North Idaho.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported 6 inches of snow in Sandpoint, Idaho.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of weather disturbances brought heavy snow at times to North Idaho.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The department of highways reported trees and power lines blown down between Soloa road and Larch Road about 4 miles south southwest of Medimont, Idaho associated with a snow storm moving through the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Clearwater-Municipal consists of a group of fires in Clearwater, Idaho and Lewis Counties in northern Idaho. The fires, started by lightning, have been burning since approximately 1:15 pm on August 10th. Fire activity blew up Friday, August 14th, after a cold front brought in strong winds, spreading flames north of Kamiah along the Woodland Ridge and Frasure Ridge roads, east to the Caribel area and south to Nikesa Creek in Church Canyon.   This resulted in 42 destroyed homes and at least 79 outbuildings in Idaho county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: KREM news reported The Lawyer Complex has burned 42 homes and 75 other buildings, according to Northern Rockies Incident Management Team.  The National situation reported  118 structures lost (as of the last report 9/10/2015) with the cost of fighting the fire at 25 million dollars. The Idaho County Sheriff's Office says a woman died preparing for evacuations due to the Lawyer Complex Fire. She died Friday evening after falling and hitting her head.
